column1: # FasTag species reference set.
# taxid <TAB> label <TAB> category
# Fetched as UniProt REFERENCE PROTEOMES (one protein per gene, complete), NOT
# `reviewed:true`. The previous set used reviewed-only and was wildly uneven --
# 20,431 proteins for human against 35 for S. pneumoniae -- because curation
# effort tracks how well-studied an organism is, not its proteome size. That
# unevenness goes straight into the background model: a taxon with 35 proteins
# has an artificially tiny expectation, so anything matching it looks enriched.
# Scope is deliberately breadth-first, not depth: short peptide tags resolve
# genus/family at best, so one good representative per genus beats several
# strains of the same species.
# ---- MS contaminants / reagent sources ------------------------------------
# Present in almost every LC-MS/MS run regardless of sample. Omitting them does
# not make them go away; it makes their peptides land on whatever taxon is
# closest, which is worse than naming them.
# ---- plant pathogens (the original target lineage) -------------------------
# ---- archaea (breadth, so the tree is not bacteria-only) -------------------
# Strain-level taxids: the SPECIES taxids (2190, 2287) carry only non-reference
# proteomes, so a species-level fetch silently returns nothing.
